0.original: A little over two years ago, a young man asked me to help him find a job with more future.

1.original: He was employed as a clerk in the credit department of a mail order company and felt that he was getting nowhere.

2.original: We talked about his past record and what he wanted to do.

3.original: After knowing something about him, I said, I admire you very much for wanting to move up the ladder to a better job and more responsibility.

4.original: But getting a start in the kind of job you want requires a college degree these days.

5.original: I notice you've finished three semesters.

6.original: May I suggest you finish college?

7.original: Going summers, you can do it in two years.

8.original: Then I'm sure you can land the job you want, with the company you want to work for." I realize,

9.original: he answered, that a college education would help, but it's impossible for me to go back to school.

13.original: Well, for one thing, he began, I'm twenty-four.

14.original: On top of that, my wife and I are expecting our second child in a couple of months.

15.original: We barely get by now on what I make.

16.original: I wouldn't have time to study since I'd have to keep my job.

18.original: This young man really had himself convinced that finishing college was impossible.

19.original: Then I said to him, if you believe it is impossible to finish school, then it is.

20.original: But by the same token, if you'll just believe it is possible to return to the university,

22.original: Now here's what I would like you to do.

23.original: Make up your mind you are going to go back to school.

24.original: Let that one thought dominate your thinking.

25.original: Then think, really think about how you can do it and still support your family.

26.original: Come back in a couple of weeks and let me know what ideas you've come up with." My young friend returned two weeks later.

27.original: I thought a lot about what you said, he began.

28.original: I've decided I must go back to school.

29.original: I haven't figured out all the angles yet, but I'll find a solution.

31.original: He managed to get a scholarship provided by a trade association, which paid his tuition, books, and incidentals.

32.original: He rearranged his work schedule so he could attend classes.

33.original: His enthusiasm and the promise of a better life won him his wife's full support.

34.original: Together they creatively found ways to budget money and time more effectively.

35.original: Last month, he received his degree one day and went to work the next as a management trainee for a large corporation.

36.original: Where there's a will, there is a way.
